title: Spears of Longinus
wikitext:
[]: Spears of Longinus             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Mon Sep 08, 2008 4:38 pm As topic, how exactly do SEELE manage to obtain or create those ten or so replica Spears which play so crucial a role in EoE? Was it something inherent in the the Dead Sea Scrolls which taught them how to do it, or is it another example of inexplicable amazing Evaverse cloning (the Spears supposedly living beings and stuff), or both? It's been said that the Spears are self-propelled and have a will of their own, so what exactly are they thinking while flying along that single dimension they know? What goes through those experienced, veteran linear minds of theirs, simple though they may be? One idea I had was that they meant to serve the FAR's purposes, since...well, that's what they were designed to do. Security restraining bolts, intended to keep the Seeds under control in case anything went wrong. That seems perfectly fine for the original "red" Spear meeting Arael and going, "OK I'm gonna kill ya now." Again the same Spear comes all the way back from the moon to stop short at Yui-sama's neck...presumably another desperate (failed) attempt to stop 3I occurring(?). But what about the grey ones, wielded by the Mass Production Evas, which all go off and kill Asuka who'd otherwise have done such a good job to prevent 3I? Were these engineered by SEELE to further their own ulterior motive? Is there any other explanation for their supposed "murder" of Asuka on EoE? Or am I reading too far into the whole thing, when in fact it was just doing so by being thrown from Unit-08? Talking about this makes me think it must be an exceedingly boring life to be a Spear...

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Sep 08, 2008 5:12 pm Reverse-Engineering. And the spear is a living thing, so they probably managed to clone it somehow. I mean, they cloned Adam and whatnot.

[]: Re: Spears of Longinus             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 08, 2008 11:22 pm Yes -- cloning. The change in color is "consistent" with the fact that none of the "clones" in the show are chromatically identical to the originals. Ayr wrote: so what exactly are they thinking while flying along that single dimension they know?

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Sep 09, 2008 7:57 am Hmm it does seem to make more sense having the spear flying at Asuka spontaneously. But does the series make any comments as to what they are made of, and what mechanism it is that helps them change shape so drastically, as well as being self-propelled?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 09, 2008 11:28 am The series itself? I don't think it says very much about the Spear of Longinus, or its derivatives, at all. Keeping things vague makes them more "versatile", after all.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Sep 09, 2008 12:18 pm Alright, well, let's extend that to believable-tier canon, then. ANYTHING ?!

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Sep 09, 2008 1:01 pm If they're living organisms, probably PWM. Which has magical shapeshifting AT-Field powrz.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Sep 09, 2008 1:36 pm Oh, no, not that stuff again... I used to entertain the idea of the LnY having an AT Field of its own, and hence being capable of self-propulsion much like the Angels, but it all seems slightly awkward a proposition. Although why not, if they're living beings themselves? This would mean that they break through others' AT Fields by eroding them with their own, right...? If I rant on much longer I will end up , so I now formally request to be made out of PWM, that amazing shtuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 09, 2008 1:50 pm AyrYntake wrote: Oh, no, not that stuff again...

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Sep 09, 2008 1:55 pm Reichu wrote: Making the Spears ensouled beings also complicates things an awful lot.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:36 pm Best thing I can think of is that their artificial nature is taken to an extreme, so their structure and basic functioning is based upon biological principles, so they're "alive", but they're designed to function without souls -- for whatever reason -- and maybe just have some facsimile thereof instead (e.g., MAGI or dummy system, but better); how they can penetrate ATFs, only FAR knows. This probably raises more questions than it answers, but, then, making the Spears ensouled beings also complicates things an awful lot.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:56 pm AuraTwilight wrote: AyrYntake wrote: True. It would mean they were getting involved in 3I for one. And we seriously don't want that.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 10, 2008 10:15 pm No, but their souls would have to be involved in the Instrumentality Process, which means you'd have random killer Spears of Doom talking to you all the while during 3I, which would complicate matters to the nth degree...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Sep 10, 2008 10:17 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Yea, because the Angels had SO MUCH psychological depth.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Sep 11, 2008 2:38 am AuraTwilight wrote: Yea, because the Angels had SO MUCH psychological depth.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 11, 2008 1:44 pm I wouldn't use Kaworu as a testament to Angel psychologies, since he's not really the same type of being and he's 15 years older than all the rest anyway, but the Angels are essentially big lonely babies. They have psychologies, but depth? Not so much. Besides, CI, and the existence of Eva Units, proves that AT Fields are about "size" or some bullshit, not what's in the mind.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Sep 11, 2008 3:47 pm AT Field strength is SO not about size. I pondered this at the start of the "AT Field Technicalities" thread. Counting out Kaworu, consider the difference in AT Field strength between Matariel and Zeruel. It's the wall within our hearts, as Kaworu so distinctly pointed out near the end. Now if that doesn't require psychological depth, I don't know what else does. Let's assume, for the sake of argument, that it IS to do with size. Even THEN the LnY would have trouble functioning since it's by no means bigger than Arael, and in terms of volume it's still not bigger than Kyoko-sama. Hence, the idea that the LnY has an AT Field, or a soul for that matter, does not stand up to close scrutiny.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 12, 2008 1:58 am AT Field strength is SO not about size. I pondered this at the start of the "AT Field Technicalities" thread. Counting out Kaworu, consider the difference in AT Field strength between Matariel and Zeruel. It's the wall within our hearts, as Kaworu so distinctly pointed out near the end. Now if that doesn't require psychological depth, I don't know what else does.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Sep 12, 2008 2:17 am Agreed. It definitely has a will of its own.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 12, 2008 1:53 pm And thus an apparent soul and AT Field. Problem solved.
